title: strychnos variabilis
intro: Strychnos variabilis, also known as the variable strychnine tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Loganiaceae. This plant contains strychnine and brucine, toxic alkaloids with medicinal properties that have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Strychnos variabilis include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves or bark may be used to make herbal preparations, including teas and tonics.
